summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service by reading Isaiah 43:1-2, declaring a message titled 'This Is What the Lord Says.' He began by acknowledging the 'giant voices' many face—fear, discouragement, hopelessness—shouting about failed marriages, addiction, anxiety, and illness. He then shared his personal testimony of suffering a severe hemorrhagic stroke 15 months prior, where doctors warned of irreversible damage, but he stood as a testament to God's power to 'reverse the irreversible.' The core message was that God's word is the ultimate authority over any other voice. Pastor Travis offered three pieces of encouragement from the Isaiah text. First, 'Do not fear,' because God gives a peace the world cannot offer. He illustrated this with the story of David and Goliath, emphasizing that David's courage came from focusing on God's size, not the giant's, and that enemy voices only come when you are a threat. Second, 'You are going to get through it.' He highlighted the word 'when' in Isaiah, indicating our trials are temporary. The 'pass through' stage, though difficult, is where faith is tested and power is developed, likening it to the waiting in Isaiah 40:31 that renews strength and allows us to 'soar like eagles' instead of acting in fear 'like chickens.' Third, 'God is going to go through it with you.' He paralleled Isaiah's promise with Psalm 23, reminding the church that Jesus is the Good Shepherd who is with us even in the 'valley of the shadow of death,' which is just a shadow because the light of Christ is present. He concluded with an altar call, inviting those who had not yet given their lives to Jesus to do so, and a prayer of gratitude for God's faithful word and presence in our passing through.
mainPoints: Do not fear the 'giant voices' of life, but listen to and trust in what the Lord says, finding peace in His authority.
You will get through your trial; the 'pass through' stage is temporary and is where your faith is tested and your spiritual power is developed.
God promises to go through the difficulty with you; as your Shepherd, He is present even in the darkest valleys, which are only shadows in His light.

versesMentioned.text: But now, this is what the Lord says— he who created you, Jacob, he who formed you, Israel: 'Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have summoned you by name; you are mine. When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned; the flames will not set you ablaze.'
so is my word that goes out from my mouth: It will not return to me empty, but will accomplish what I desire and achieve the purpose for which I sent it.
Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.

David asked the men standing near him, 'What will be done for the man who kills this Philistine and removes this disgrace from Israel? Who is this uncircumcised Philistine that he should defy the armies of the living God?'
David said to Saul, 'Let no one lose heart on account of this Philistine; your servant will go and fight him.'
Praise the Lord, my soul, and forget not all his benefits—
I will remember the deeds of the Lord; yes, I will remember your miracles of long ago.
but those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint.

The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
I am the good shepherd; I know my sheep and my sheep know me—
My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me.
When Jesus spoke again to the people, he said, 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.'

Monday.scripture: Isaiah 43:1-2 (NIV) - 'Do not fear, for I have redeemed you...'
Monday.focus: God's promise of presence in difficulty.
Monday.action: Identify one 'giant voice' of fear or discouragement you are hearing today. Write it down, then write 'This is what the Lord says:' next to it, followed by a promise from Scripture.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that you are with me. Help me to silence the voice of fear by listening to your voice of truth today.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Samuel 17:32 (NIV) - 'Let no one lose heart... your servant will go and fight him.'
Tuesday.focus: Courage in the face of giants.
Tuesday.action: Recall a past challenge where God gave you victory. Write it down as a reminder of His faithfulness for your current situation.
Tuesday.prayer: God, give me the courage of David. Remind me of your past faithfulness so I can face today's challenge with confidence in you.

Wednesday.scripture: Isaiah 40:31 (NIV) - '...those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength.'
Wednesday.action: Evaluate your response to a current difficulty. Are you reacting in fear ('like a chicken') or in faith ('like an eagle')? Choose one practical step of faith to take today.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, in this waiting period, renew my strength. Help me to wait on you with hope and to soar above my circumstances by faith.

Thursday.scripture: Psalm 23:4 (NIV) - 'Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me.'
Thursday.action: Memorize Psalm 23:4. Throughout the day, when you feel alone or afraid, quietly recite this verse to yourself.
Thursday.prayer: Shepherd, thank you for walking with me. When I am in the shadowy places, help me to remember that you are right beside me, guiding and comforting me.

Friday.scripture: John 10:27 (NIV) - 'My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me.'
Friday.focus: Discerning and following the Shepherd's voice.
Friday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet prayer today, asking God to help you distinguish His voice from the many other voices. Journal any scripture or thought that comes to mind.
Friday.prayer: Good Shepherd, train my ear to recognize your voice above all others. Help me to stop following the crowd and to follow you alone.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 77:11 (NIV) - 'I will remember the deeds of the Lord; yes, I will remember your miracles of long ago.'
Saturday.action: Create a 'Miracles of God' list on your phone or in a journal. Start by writing down at least three specific times God has helped, healed, or provided for you.
Saturday.prayer: God, I choose to remember your wonderful deeds. Let my list of your miracles build my faith for the future you have for me.
